data.body: BLOCKER / DECISION POINT (2026-07-12). Claim phase reached a hard, well-characterized wall. State: 6/10 claimed (epic, ac0e[committed 0fb8d17e6, docs], e1f2, a37b, f32e, 24ec); 5 open+stuck (74d9, a8e5, d4cf, 9f25, b9c0). The 5 stuck leaves are in a NON-CONVERGENT plan-review claim gate (finding recorded as epic comment #1): verdict instability on identical plans (74d9/a8e5 INDETERMINATE->BLOCK unchanged), non-monotonic block counts across 4+ rounds each, new-code grounding INDETERMINATE, and per-round test-blast-radius churn. Every individual block was valid+sharp and was honestly remediated; the gate just does not converge for these complex/dependency-coupled leaves (b9c0's threshold genuinely depends on 9f25's not-yet-run derivation). Cannot claim without a PASS (fail-closed); cannot  without operator approval (CLAUDE.local.md). AWAITING OPERATOR DECISION: (A) approve claim --force with documented audit reasons for the 5 stuck leaves so implementation can proceed; or (B) descope/split the 5 (esp. b9c0/d4cf) into their own effort; or (C) keep iterating the gate (may not terminate). No commits beyond ac0e; HEAD 0fb8d17e6; worktree /Users/joeoakhart/rebar-epic-9d50. Fully resumable.
